title: Chapter 1 Things Every Driver Should Know

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, why might someone without mechanical skills feel exasperated watching others work on cars?
options: A. Others charge more for repairs
B. Others understand how things work and fit together
C. Others use expensive tools
D. Others refuse to help

mcq.q: What is the author's main argument about learning car maintenance?
options: A. It requires innate mechanical talent
B. It is only for professional mechanics
C. It can be learned without being 'born with a wrench'
D. It is too dangerous for beginners

mcq.q: Why does the author recommend learning to fill the fuel tank yourself?
options: A. To avoid paying for professional service
B. To save money compared to full-service
C. To prevent fuel leaks
D. To learn about fuel types

mcq.q: What is the first question the author suggests asking before tackling a car maintenance job?
options: A. Do I have the correct tools?
B. Do I really want to do this?
C. Can I afford to pay a mechanic?
D. Is it safe to start?

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what is a key reason to know how to open the hood of a car?
options: A. To check the oil or coolant level
B. To avoid lockouts
C. To replace the windshield
D. To install a new radio

mcq.q: What does the excerpt state about the simplest car tasks?
options: A. They require specialized tools
B. They can be the biggest hurdles to overcome
C. They should always be done by professionals
D. They are unnecessary for new drivers

mcq.q: What does the author mention as a necessary step before changing a tire?
options: A. Checking the tire pressure
B. Jacking up the vehicle safely
C. Removing the hubcap
D. Cleaning the wheel

mcq.q: What does the excerpt say about terms in a specific font?
options: A. They refer to common car models
B. They are defined in Appendix A
C. They indicate safety warnings
D. They are technical jargon

mcq.q: Why does the author include instructions for 'taking things apart and putting them back together'?
options: A. To teach advanced engineering
B. To provide a surefire method for disassembly
C. To replace professional mechanics
D. To avoid using safety gear

mcq.q: What does the excerpt imply about the cost of tools for self-repair?
options: A. Always buy new expensive tools
B. Borrow or rent tools if possible
C. Only use tools from dealerships
D. Avoid tools to save money

mcq.q: According to the excerpt, what should you consider before attempting a vehicle repair yourself?
options: A) Whether your vehicle is currently insured
B) Whether the job passes the test of time, cost, and safety
C) Whether your mechanic is available
D) Whether the part is on sale

mcq.q: What key lesson did the author learn from 'the big guys' about loosening stuck bolts?
options: A) Strength is determined by size and gender
B) Longer handles provide more leverage
C) Professionals always have the right tools
D) Patience is unnecessary for quick fixes

mcq.q: What should you do if a repair job doesn't pass the self-repair cost/benefit test?
options: A) Rush to complete it before it gets harder
B) Turn it over to a professional after verifying necessity
C) Skip the repair entirely
D) Buy cheaper parts to save money

mcq.q: What is the most annoying part of automotive repairs mentioned in the excerpt?
options: A) Finding the right tools
B) Disabling the vehicle to work on it
C) Returning to the store for incorrect parts
D) Explaining the problem to a mechanic

mcq.q: How often did the author experience needing to return to the store for incorrect parts before learning proper buying tips?
options: A) Once every three jobs
B) One out of every three times
C) Two out of every three times
D) Almost never

mcq.q: What does the excerpt say about focusing strength when using tools?
options: A) It should be directed upward
B) It depends on size or sex
C) It should be poured down arms into hands
D) It requires shouting to be effective

mcq.q: What should you verify before trusting a professional for a vehicle repair?
options: A) Their hourly rate
B) Whether the job is necessary and properly done
C) Their availability for weekend work
D) The color of their uniform

mcq.q: What does the excerpt warn against when buying replacement parts?
options: A) Buying parts online
B) Not reading the section on buying parts
C) Using generic parts
D) Buying parts without a diagram

mcq.q: What does the excerpt state is the primary factor in loosening stuck bolts, not strength?
options: A) Using a larger hammer
B) The way strength is focused
C) Having a mechanic present
D) The time of day

mcq.q: What is the main purpose of Chapter 3 as mentioned in the excerpt?
options: A) To teach driving safety
B) To explain how to buy specific tools
C) To cover advanced engine repairs
D) To discuss insurance claims

mcq.q: Where are vehicle specifications often printed besides the owner's manual?
options: A. On the dashboard display
B. On metal tags or decals inside the hood
C. On the vehicle's license plate
D. On the fuel pump nozzle

mcq.q: What does OEM stand for in the context of vehicle parts?
options: A. Original Equipment Manufacturer
B. Original Engine Model
C. Over-the-Counter Mechanic
D. Engine Optimization Method

mcq.q: Where should you record vehicle specifications for future reference?
options: A. In the vehicle's service manual
B. In Appendix B of the textbook
C. On the vehicle's VIN plate
D. On the dealership's invoice

mcq.q: Where can you find spark plug specifications if not in the owner's manual?
options: A. On the vehicle's tire pressure label
B. In a 'Tune-Up Specification Guide' at an auto supply store
C. In the vehicle's warranty booklet
D. On the manufacturer's website homepage

mcq.q: What is a recommended strategy when buying parts to avoid labor charges?
options: A. Always buy parts online without consulting a store
B. Ask for a discount as a DIY installer
C. Purchase only aftermarket parts for lower cost
D. Buy all parts at dealership without asking for discounts

mcq.q: Why should you prefer OEM parts over aftermarket parts?
options: A. OEM parts are always cheaper
B. OEM parts match the vehicle's original specifications
C. Aftermarket parts are harder to find
D. OEM parts include labor discounts

mcq.q: Where might a vehicle's ID decal be located according to the text?
options: A. Under the driver's seat
B. Inside the glove compartment lid
C. On the rear bumper
D. On the vehicle's tire sidewall

mcq.q: What should you do if you cannot find spark plug specs in your vehicle's documentation?
options: A. Assume standard plug size for all vehicles
B. Consult a 'Tune-Up Specification Guide' at an auto supply store
C. Use the same plugs as your neighbor's car
D. Ignore the specification and install any plugs

mcq.q: What information do vehicle ID tags typically provide besides specs?
options: A. Fuel efficiency ratings
B. Manufacturing location and paint type
C. Current odometer reading
D. Warranty expiration date

mcq.q: What is a common misconception about vehicle maintenance mentioned in the text?
options: A. Maintenance causes engine explosions
B. Safety rules are unnecessary for DIY work
C. Making a small mistake will cause an explosion
D. Parts must always be bought new

mcq.q: What should you do before working on a vehicle's engine to avoid burns?
options: A) Run the engine for 10 minutes
B) Ensure parts are cool or follow warm engine precautions
C) Use gasoline to clean the engine
D) Work with the parking brake off

mcq.q: Why is it dangerous to store gasoline in a regular can?
options: A) It evaporates too quickly
B) Gasoline vapor can form an explosive mixture with air
C) It causes rust on the can
D) It makes the car harder to start

mcq.q: How should you safely dispose of an empty gasoline can?
options: A) Pour it down a drain
B) Fill with water and recycle at a toxic waste center
C) Burn it in a controlled fire
D) Store it under the car

mcq.q: What must be done before jacking a vehicle?
options: A) Turn on the engine
B) Block the wheels properly
C) Remove the parking brake
D) Use unblocked wheels

mcq.q: Why should you avoid using gasoline to clean parts?
options: A) It is more expensive than solvent
B) Mechanic's solvent is safer and flame-retardant
C) It leaves residue on parts
D) It requires special tools

mcq.q: What safety rule applies to electrical work on a vehicle?
options: A) Always use bare hands
B) Use insulated tools
C) Work with the engine running
D) Wear metal jewelry

mcq.q: What should you do to prevent injury when using a wrench on a stuck part?
options: A) Push the wrench forcefully
B) Pull the wrench instead of pushing
C) Wear gloves only
D) Work with one hand

mcq.q: Why should you remove jewelry before working on a car?
options: A) It prevents rust on the jewelry
B) It could get caught on parts and cause injury
C) It makes the car look cleaner
D) It reduces tool weight

mcq.q: What is the correct parking procedure before working on a vehicle?
options: A) Park in Drive with engine running
B) Parking brake off, gear in Reverse
C) Parking brake on, gear in Park or Neutral, engine off
D) Engine on, gear in Drive

mcq.q: What should you never do while working on a vehicle?
options: A) Use a flashlight
B) Smoke
C) Wear safety glasses
D) Check engine temperature

mcq.q: What should you do after using toxic chemicals?
options: Store them in a regular container.
Wash your hands thoroughly and store safely away from pets and children or dispose safely.
Dispose of them in the sink.
Keep them near children for safety.

mcq.q: Why is gasoline considered extremely dangerous?
options: It is only flammable.
It is toxic, flammable, and its vapor in an empty can is explosive.
It is only toxic.
It is not dangerous if stored in a plastic bottle.

mcq.q: How should you store a small amount of gasoline for a lawn mower?
options: In any container.
In a ventilated gasoline can designed specifically for that purpose.
In a plastic bottle.
In an empty water bottle.

mcq.q: Where is the best place to work on your vehicle to avoid breathing toxic fumes?
options: In a closed garage.
In a well-ventilated area, preferably outdoors.
In a basement.
In a room with no windows.

mcq.q: What should you use in dark areas during auto repair?
options: A regular flashlight.
A work light.
Your phone's flashlight.
Work without any light.

mcq.q: Why is it recommended to fill up gasoline in the morning?
options: Because gas stations are less crowded.
Because gasoline expands with heat, so cooler fuel has more energy per gallon.
Because the owner of the gas station is more likely to be there.
Because the price is lower in the morning.

mcq.q: What should you avoid doing with a gasoline can in your vehicle?
options: Storing it in the trunk.
Carrying it in or on your vehicle (unless going far into the wilds).
Using it to clean the engine.
Leaving it in the sun.

mcq.q: What is the danger of gasoline vapor in an empty can?
options: It is not dangerous.
It is explosive enough to take out a city block.
It is non-flammable.
It is only dangerous if the can is full.

mcq.q: What should you do with your hands after handling toxic chemicals?
options: Wipe them on your clothes.
Wash them thoroughly.
Use hand sanitizer.
Leave them without washing.

mcq.q: Why should you keep a fire extinguisher handy?
options: Because auto repair is always dangerous.
Chapter 3 contains reasons for this precaution that may surprise you.
To put out fires from the engine.
To put out fires from the garage.

mcq.q: Why should you avoid flames near the fuel pump?
options: A. Flames make fuel flow faster
B. Flames can ignite gasoline fumes causing an explosion
C. Flames cool the gasoline
D. Flames prevent automatic shut-off

mcq.q: What should you do if the price window shows a previous sale?
options: A. Ignore it and start pumping immediately
B. Reset it by inserting a credit card or having an attendant clear the machine
C. Wait for the price to disappear on its own
D. Select the fuel grade before resetting

mcq.q: What is the purpose of depressing the rubber vapor-recovery sleeve on some nozzles?
options: A. To prevent automatic shut-off
B. To allow fuel to flow into the tank
C. To measure fuel volume accurately
D. To cool the fuel before pumping

mcq.q: What happens when the pump automatically shuts off?
options: A. The trigger latch releases immediately
B. The numbers in the pump window stop moving and the trigger clicks closed
C. You must manually stop the flow
D. The credit card receipt prints automatically

mcq.q: Why should you never 'top off' a tank after the pump shuts off?
options: A. It reduces fuel efficiency
B. It causes the pump to malfunction
C. Fuel may overflow, ruining asphalt and creating a fire hazard
D. It prevents automatic shut-off from working

mcq.q: What should you do after removing the nozzle from the fuel tank?
options: A. Immediately replace the fuel cap while the receipt prints
B. Check tire pressure before hanging the nozzle
C. Wash the windows while the pump is still running
D. Press 'Yes' for a receipt before hanging the nozzle

mcq.q: What is the correct step before selecting the fuel grade?
options: A. Unscrew the fuel tank cap
B. Insert the credit card into the slot
C. Move the pump lever to ON
D. Reset the price window if necessary

mcq.q: What is the purpose of the trigger latch on the pump nozzle?
options: A. To increase fuel flow speed
B. To prevent automatic shut-off
C. To allow you to step away from the pump and avoid fumes
D. To measure the exact amount of fuel

mcq.q: What should you do if the price window does not reset to $0.00?
options: A. Proceed to select fuel grade immediately
B. Try inserting your credit card or have the attendant clear the machine
C. Wait for the pump to start automatically
D. Skip the credit card step and use cash

mcq.q: Why is overfilling a tank especially dangerous on hot days?
options: A. Heat makes fuel less flammable
B. Heat causes fuel to expand, increasing overflow risk
C. Heat makes the pump shut off too early
D. Heat reduces vapor-recovery sleeve effectiveness

mcq.q: Where is the hood release typically located in newer vehicle models?
options: Behind the front grill
Near the steering column or driver's seat
Under the dashboard
On the passenger side door

mcq.q: What is the purpose of the safety catch after popping the hood open?
options: To prevent the hood from opening while driving
To secure the hood during high-speed driving
To hold the hood closed during rain
To make the hood easier to close

mcq.q: If you cannot locate the hood release, what should you do according to the text?
options: Use a screwdriver to pry it open
Ask a gas station attendant for assistance
Wait until the next mechanic visit
Check the owner's manual online

mcq.q: What is the recommended alternative to paying for full service when asking for hood assistance?
options: Request only a tire pressure check
Ask for $5 worth of gasoline
Get a free oil change
Skip the tip entirely

mcq.q: How should you operate the hood release if it is located at the front of the vehicle?
options: Push it forward only
Press, push, or pull it from front to back and side to side
Twist it clockwise
Pull it upward firmly

mcq.q: What does the text say about checking oil yourself instead of having an attendant do it?
options: It is more expensive
It is covered by a sidebar in Chapter 2
It requires special tools
It is only for older vehicles

mcq.q: What should you do after the hood pops open but is stopped by the safety catch?
options: Ignore it and drive away
Press the safety catch one way or the other
Call roadside assistance
Use a hammer to release it

mcq.q: Where is the hood release typically located in older vehicle models?
options: Near the steering column
Inside the glove compartment
Behind the grill or bumper
On the roof of the car

mcq.q: What should you do if you still cannot open the hood after reading the instructions?
options: Try jiggling the steering wheel
Consult the owner's manual again
Head to the full-service bay for help
Use the emergency brake

mcq.q: What is mentioned as a benefit of getting your windows washed and tire pressure checked when asking for hood help?
options: It is free
It saves money on oil changes
It is only available at night
It requires no additional payment

mcq.q: What should you do if the hood doesn't stay up securely after release?
options: A. Ignore it and drive away
B. Secure it with a broom handle or similar object
C. Immediately replace the entire hood
D. Apply adhesive to the hinges

mcq.q: Where is a hood prop typically attached?
options: A. To the car's dashboard
B. To the engine block
C. To the underside of the hood or bottom edge of the hood opening
D. To the windshield wipers

mcq.q: What danger is associated with gas-pressurized hood shocks?
options: A. They can overheat while driving
B. Gas may leak, causing the hood to fall
C. They require frequent oiling
D. They emit toxic fumes

mcq.q: What should you do before removing any part of a vehicle?
options: A. Check the owner's manual for part numbers
B. Ask yourself four specific questions
C. Clean the part with solvent
D. Take a photo of the assembly

mcq.q: According to the text, what is the first step when taking something apart?
options: A. Remove all screws immediately
B. Lay a clean, lint-free rag on a flat surface
C. Turn off the car engine
D. Gather all tools in one place

mcq.q: Why should you avoid distractions while taking something apart?
options: A. To prevent noise complaints
B. To reduce the risk of dropping parts
C. To maintain focus and avoid panic
D. To comply with safety regulations

mcq.q: What should you NOT do when taking something apart?
options: A. Take your time
B. Drink coffee to relax
C. Work in a hurry
D. Make notes of answers to key questions

mcq.q: What is the purpose of the rag mentioned in the disassembly instructions?
options: A. To absorb oil spills
B. To prevent parts from falling
C. To provide a clean surface for parts
D. To clean the work area

mcq.q: Which question is NOT listed as one to ask before disassembly?
options: A. What is this thing?
B. How tightly is it screwed?
C. What does it do?
D. Why is it made the way it is?

mcq.q: What is the recommended action if hood shocks feel insecure?
options: A. Tighten the hood screws
B. Secure with a broom handle and have them checked
C. Replace the hood immediately
D. Apply lubricant to the shocks

mcq.q: Why shouldn't bolts holding gaskets be tightened too securely?
options: A. It prevents the gasket from being visible
B. It avoids stripped threads or deformed gaskets
C. It makes removal easier later
D. It reduces the need for torque specifications

mcq.q: When removing parts, how should they be placed on the rag?
options: A. In random order with no direction
B. Counterclockwise with parts pointing inward
C. Clockwise with parts pointing in original direction
D. In alphabetical order by part name

mcq.q: What is the purpose of numbering parts during removal?
options: A. To identify expensive parts
B. To track the sequence for reassembly
C. To match parts to a parts catalog
D. To calculate torque specifications

mcq.q: How should parts be reassembled?
options: A. In the same order they were removed
B. Starting with the first part removed
C. Counterclockwise from the last removed part
D. Randomly based on size

mcq.q: What risk occurs if a gasket bolt is over-tightened?
options: A. The bolt becomes too loose
B. The gasket may be squeezed out of shape
C. The car's paint gets scratched
D. The tire loses air pressure

mcq.q: What note should be made about parts that shouldn't be over-tightened?
options: A. 'Use lubricant'
B. 'Don't over-tighten'
C. 'Requires special tools'
D. 'Check for rust'

mcq.q: What does the placement and direction of parts on the rag indicate?
options: A. The cost of each part
B. When to reassemble and orientation
C. The manufacturer of the part
D. The weight of each part

mcq.q: How can you prevent having extra nuts/bolts at the end of a job?
options: A. By skipping the numbering step
B. By working systematically and understanding part function
C. By using only the largest tools
D. By removing all parts at once

mcq.q: What is a primary reason to jack up a car?
options: A. To clean the engine
B. To change a tire or inspect brakes
C. To test the suspension
D. To replace the windshield

mcq.q: What is essential when jacking a car, according to the text?
options: A. A creeper
B. Jack stands
C. A tire pressure gauge
D. An oil filter

mcq.q: What should you never do with a jack?
options: Use it to lift the vehicle
Use it to hold the vehicle in place
Use it without jack stands
Use it on an incline

mcq.q: Why must jack stands be used when working under a vehicle?
options: To make the vehicle easier to lift
To prevent injury or death if the vehicle falls
To secure the wheels during tire changes
To hold the vehicle on a hill

mcq.q: What should you use to block wheels when jacking up a vehicle?
options: Only the parking brake
Bricks, wooden wedges, or metal wheel chocks
The transmission in Park
The emergency brake only

mcq.q: What is the recommended action for a flat tire on a highway?
options: Change the tire on the shoulder
Exit the vehicle and change the tire
Use a cellphone to call for help
Drive slowly to the nearest exit

mcq.q: What is recommended to keep in the vehicle for emergencies?
options: A spare tire
A cellphone for emergency calls
Jack stands
Wheel chocks

mcq.q: What should you do if you have a flat on a hill without wheel blocks?
options: Park on the hill and change the tire without blocking
Park near the curb, turn wheels toward curb, and block downside wheels
Drive to the bottom of the hill to change the tire
Change the tire without parking

mcq.q: Why is changing a tire on a freeway dangerous?
options: It causes tire damage
You can be injured and fall prey to carjackers
The vehicle may overheat
It requires more tools

mcq.q: What should you do if you have no cellphone during a flat tire emergency?
options: Change the tire quickly
Hang a white rag or white paper out the window
Run to the nearest building
Signal with headlights

mcq.q: What must be done before jacking up a vehicle?
options: Turn off the engine
Park on level ground
Have a spare tire ready
Check tire pressure

mcq.q: What gearshift position should be used before jacking up a vehicle?
options: Drive
Neutral
Park (or First for manual)
Reverse

mcq.q: When should the parking brake NOT be engaged?
options: A) When changing a tire
B) When inspecting brakes by removing rear brake drums
C) When driving on a highway
D) When jacking up the car

mcq.q: What happens if you work under a car without securely jacking and blocking it?
options: A) The car will not start
B) The vehicle can cause damage to itself and you if it falls
C) The tires will deflate
D) The engine will overheat

mcq.q: Why must lug nuts be loosened before jacking up the car?
options: A) To prevent the wheel from falling off
B) Because the wheel turns freely after jacking, making it hard to loosen
C) To check tire pressure
D) To remove the wheel cover

mcq.q: What should you do if you lack an owner's manual for jack placement?
options: A) Guess the correct position
B) Use the rear axle for all repairs
C) Ask the service department at a dealership
D) Jack up the car without blocking

mcq.q: What is the safe practice for two-wheel repairs?
options: A) Always jack up both wheels simultaneously
B) Place the jack near the rear axle without blocking
C) Stick to jacking one wheel at a time until proficient
D) Skip blocking the wheels

mcq.q: What should you do before jacking up a car for tire or brake work?
options: A) Check the oil level
B) Remove the wheel cover and loosen lug nuts
C) Start the engine
D) Inflate the tires

mcq.q: What is the danger of placing the jack where weight rests on bendable parts?
options: A) The car will accelerate faster
B) You can injure the car
C) The tires will burst
D) The engine will stall

mcq.q: Why must front wheels be blocked when the parking brake is off for brake inspection?
options: A) To prevent the car from rolling
B) To make rear brake drums easier to remove
C) To allow rear wheel rotation
D) To secure the jack

mcq.q: What is the correct sequence for removing a wheel?
options: A) Jack up, loosen lug nuts, remove wheel cover
B) Loosen lug nuts, remove wheel cover, jack up
C) Remove wheel cover, jack up, loosen lug nuts
D) Jack up, remove wheel cover, loosen lug nuts

mcq.q: When is it acceptable to place the jack near the rear axle?
options: A) Always for all repairs
B) Only for two-wheel repairs after gaining proficiency
C) Only for one-wheel repairs
D) Never

mcq.q: What is the correct technique for using a hydraulic jack?
options: A. Pump rapidly with short strokes
B. Use nice, even strokes from lowest to highest point
C. Pump only upward without lowering
D. Use forceful jerking motions

mcq.q: Why is it dangerous to substitute boxes, stones, or bricks for jack stands?
options: A. They make the vehicle too heavy to lift
B. They can slip out or break while under the car
C. They prevent the jack from working properly
D. They cause tire damage during lifting

mcq.q: What must be done before removing the jack after placing jack stands?
options: A. Check tire pressure
B. Wiggle the vehicle to ensure security
C. Lower the jack completely first
D. Tighten all lug nuts

mcq.q: What should you do if you get a flat tire in an area without cell service?
options: A. Wait for help from the roadside
B. Call a friend using a satellite phone
C. Know how to change the tire yourself
D. Drive slowly to the nearest town

mcq.q: How do you lower a hydraulic jack after changing a tire?
options: A. Turn the crank opposite direction
B. Press the release valve with the rod
C. Remove the jack stands first
D. Pump the handle upward

mcq.q: What safety step follows placing jack stands under the vehicle?
options: A. Drive the vehicle forward
B. Lock the jack stands in place
C. Remove the jack
D. Check the oil level

mcq.q: What does wiggling the vehicle test for?
options: A. Engine performance
B. Proper wheel blocking and stability
C. Tire tread depth
D. Brake functionality

mcq.q: How do you lower a scissor jack after changing a tire?
options: A. Release the pressure valve
B. Turn the crank in the opposite direction
C. Pump the handle downward
D. Remove the jack stands first

mcq.q: What should you do with the old tire and jack equipment after changing a tire?
options: A. Leave them under the car
B. Put them away
C. Throw them on the roadside
D. Store them in the trunk

mcq.q: Where should jack stands be placed relative to the jack?
options: A. Under the engine block
B. Near where the jack is touching the vehicle
C. At the rear bumper
D. On the tires

mcq.q: When should you check your spare tire and related tools?
options: Only when you have a flat tire
After you've changed a tire
Before trouble occurs if unfamiliar with your vehicle
Once a year during routine maintenance

mcq.q: What tool can be used to remove a standard wheel cover or hubcap?
options: A tire iron
A hammer
A screwdriver or lug wrench flat end
A tire pressure gauge

mcq.q: Why should you place lug nuts into the wheel cover after removal?
options: To prevent them from getting lost or rolling away
To lubricate the threads for easier reinstallation
To test their size and fit
To check for rust or damage

mcq.q: What should you do if the wheel cover has a delicate finish?
options: Use a power tool to remove it quickly
Consult the owner's manual for removal instructions
Apply lubricant before prying
Discard it and use a new cover

mcq.q: How should you handle delicate aluminum or chrome-plated lug nuts?
options: Use power tools with rubberized fittings
Always tighten them with a torque wrench
Avoid power tools to prevent scratching
Replace them with standard lug nuts immediately

mcq.q: What must you check on the spare tire periodically?
options: The tire pressure
The tread depth
The rim color
The valve stem

mcq.q: Why should you lay the wheel cover on a rag after removal?
options: To keep it clean for future use
To prevent damage to the delicate finish
To absorb any oil or grease
To make it easier to store in the trunk

mcq.q: What is the analogy used for prying off a wheel cover?
options: Opening a jar lid
Removing a bottle cap
Prying the lid off a can of paint
Taking off a shoe

mcq.q: What does the text say about power tools for delicate lug nuts?
options: They are recommended for faster removal
They can cause scratches if used
They must be used with rubberized fittings
They are the only safe option

mcq.q: Where can you find a list of tools needed for changing a tire?
options: Chapter 1
Chapter 2
Chapter 3
The owner's manual

mcq.q: What is the primary purpose of locking nuts on vehicle wheels?
options: A. To prevent wheel vibration during driving
B. To make wheels easier to remove in emergencies
C. To deter wheel thieves
D. To secure the wheel hub to the axle

mcq.q: What tool is recommended for loosening lug nuts retightened by a garage with a power tool?
options: A. Standard wrench
B. Tire iron
C. Cross-shaft lug wrench
D. Socket set

mcq.q: What should you NOT do when attempting to loosen a stubborn lug nut with a standard finish?
options: A. Use a hollow pipe over the wrench handle
B. Apply weight to the left-hand arm of the wrench
C. Use strong-arm tactics on special-finish nuts
D. Work in consecutive order

mcq.q: Why should you avoid fully removing lug nuts before jacking up the vehicle?
options: A. To prevent losing the nuts on the road
B. To maintain wheel alignment during jacking
C. To ensure the tire remains supported
D. To avoid over-tightening when replacing

mcq.q: What is the correct sequence for loosening lug nuts?
options: A. Loosen all nuts at once
B. Work in consecutive order
C. Start with the top nut only
D. Randomly loosen nuts

mcq.q: What should you do before jacking up the vehicle to change a tire?
options: A. Fully remove all lug nuts
B. Remove the spare tire from the trunk
C. Apply lubricant to the bolts
D. Check tire pressure on the spare

mcq.q: How can a hollow pipe help when loosening lug nuts?
options: A. It replaces the need for a wrench
B. It provides additional leverage
C. It prevents rust on the nuts
D. It secures the wheel during removal

mcq.q: What should you do if you encounter difficulty loosening lug nuts?
options: A. Use a hammer to strike the nuts
B. Try loosening them now before an emergency
C. Ignore the problem and proceed
D. Use a longer wrench without testing

mcq.q: What happens when pulling a flat tire off the bolts?
options: A. The tire immediately lifts off the ground
B. It suddenly clears the bolts and requires full support
C. The bolts bend under pressure
D. The spare tire becomes unstable

mcq.q: What should you purchase to make loosening lug nuts easier in the future?
options: A. A tire pressure gauge
B. A cross-shaft lug wrench
C. A wheel lock key
D. A hydraulic jack

mcq.q: What should you do after placing the spare tire on the lug bolts?
options: A. Immediately tighten the lug nuts with maximum force
B. Replace the lug nuts and tighten them by hand, giving each a jolt with the wrench
C. Use the jack to lift the vehicle off the ground
D. Replace the wheel cover immediately

mcq.q: When should you fully tighten the lug nuts?
options: A. Before placing the spare tire on the lug bolts
B. After replacing the wheel cover
C. After the vehicle is resting on the ground
D. Before lifting the vehicle with the jack

mcq.q: How should you tighten lug nuts on alloy wheels?
options: A. Tighten until the lug wrench won't move, then kick the wrench
B. Tighten until the lug wrench won't move, then press down with all your weight without jerking
C. Tighten until the wheel falls off
D. Tighten until the lug wrench moves easily

mcq.q: What should you do if you have trouble replacing a hubcap?
options: A. Hit it with a wrench or hammer
B. Place it against the wheel and whack it with the heel of your hand (cushioned by a rag)
C. Skip replacing it permanently
D. Use a hammer to tap it into place

mcq.q: Why should you replace the hubcap soon?
options: A. To prevent the wheel from falling off
B. To keep dust and dirt out of the brakes and bearings
C. To make the car look more attractive
D. To avoid damaging the lug nuts

mcq.q: Where should you put the flat tire after changing the spare?
options: A. Throw it away
B. Put it in the trunk where the spare was
C. Leave it on the side of the road
D. Give it to the next driver

mcq.q: How should you handle a wheel cover with a delicate finish?
options: A. Hit it with a wrench until secure
B. Follow the owner's manual for replacement instructions
C. Use a hammer to tap it into place
D. Skip replacing it

mcq.q: What is the recommended method for tightening standard lug nuts if insufficiently tightened?
options: A. Kick the lug wrench
B. Use a hollow pipe or step on the right-hand arm of the lug wrench
C. Hit the lug nuts with a hammer
D. Tighten until loose

mcq.q: What should you avoid doing when tightening alloy wheel lug nuts?
options: A. Pressing down on the wrench
B. Jerking or kicking the wrench
C. Using a hollow pipe
D. Stepping on the lug wrench arm

mcq.q: What does the hubcap primarily do for the vehicle?
options: A. Make the car look better
B. Keep dust and dirt out of brakes and bearings
C. Prevent wheel rotation
D. Secure the spare tire

mcq.q: What is the recommended procedure for fixing a flat tire instead of 'outside-in' repair?
options: Plugging the tire from the outside
Dismounting the tire, inspecting, fixing, and remounting
Using a temporary sealant only
Replacing the tire immediately

mcq.q: Why should patches not be used for sidewall punctures?
options: Patches are too expensive
Patches only work on tread areas
Sidewall punctures cannot be patched
Sidewall repairs require special tools

mcq.q: What should you do if you use a can of inflator/sealant for a flat tire?
options: Drive indefinitely without seeking repair
Rinse it out with gasoline immediately
Get to a service station and inform them
Use it as a permanent solution

mcq.q: What is a key requirement for environmentally friendly inflator/sealant?
options: It must be flammable
It must be rinsable with soap and water
It must be used only on tread punctures
It must be applied from the inside

mcq.q: Why do some service stations plug flats from the outside?
options: It is the most durable repair method
It is faster and cheaper for the customer
It is legally required in all states
It prevents sidewall damage

mcq.q: What is a disadvantage of run-flat tires mentioned in the text?
options: They require special wheels
They are cheaper than standard tires
They cannot be repaired
They are only for emergency use

mcq.q: When should you avoid using a can of inflator/sealant?
options: When the tire is completely deflated
When you are safe and capable of changing the tire
When the puncture is in the sidewall
When service is available within 10 minutes

mcq.q: What should you tell a service facility if you used inflator/sealant?
options: To replace the tire immediately
To use the same sealant for future repairs
To remove the sealant before fixing the tire
To avoid checking for sidewall damage

mcq.q: What is the primary purpose of hiding a spare key in an obscure location?
options: To make it easy for car thieves to find
To prevent it from falling off while driving
To make it difficult for thieves to access
To use it as a temporary lock

mcq.q: What is the main benefit of run-flat tires?
options: They allow driving with a flat to a service station
They are cheaper than standard tires
They require no tire pressure checks
They eliminate the need for spare tires

mcq.q: What is a downside of losing an electronic door opener?
options: It makes the vehicle impossible to start
It requires immediate vehicle replacement
It can take days and hundreds of dollars to replace
It automatically locks the trunk

mcq.q: What should you do if you lose your electronic door opener outside the vehicle?
options: Use a straightened wire coat hanger to open the door
Call a tow truck immediately
Locate the override switch to start the vehicle
Break the window to gain entry

mcq.q: Which method is recommended for opening doors with recessed interior locks?
options: Using a wire coat hanger
Bending a paperclip
Getting professional help
Prying with a flathead screwdriver

mcq.q: What must you have to get a new key from a locksmith?
options: The vehicle's VIN number
The key code number and personal identification
The original dealer's contact information
A photo of the lost key

mcq.q: Where should you store the key code number for emergencies?
options: On a sticky note inside the glove compartment
Written where someone at home can read it and in your wallet
Memorized without writing it down
On the vehicle's dashboard

mcq.q: What is a limitation of the wire coat hanger technique?
options: It only works if the trunk is open
It requires a special tool from the dealer
It only works for old-style window ledge buttons
It damages the vehicle's paint

mcq.q: What can you do if you lock yourself out while the trunk is open?
options: Use the ignition key to start the engine
Access the rear of the car by moving the seat
Call the dealership for a new key
Use the electronic door opener

mcq.q: Why might a dealer have the key code number?
options: They always store it for every vehicle
If the vehicle was purchased new or used from them
Only for electronic keys
If the car has a digital key system

mcq.q: What is true about using a locksmith if your key is lost?
options: They can only make keys for new vehicles
They can analyze a key in good condition to find the code
They require the vehicle's manufacturer to approve the key
They always need the original key to duplicate it

mcq.q: What should you do if you have an electronic door opener but lose it outside the vehicle?
options: Try to start the ignition without it
Assume the ignition will fail to start without it
Use the hanger technique on the door button
Break the window to access the interior

short.q: What is the author's primary message about developing mechanical skills for car maintenance?
short.answer: You don't have to be born with mechanical talent; it can be learned through basic tasks.

short.q: List the four pre-job questions the author recommends asking before starting any car maintenance task.
short.answer: Do I really want to do this? Do I know how to do it? Does it require expensive tools? Can I handle mistakes?

short.q: Why does the author emphasize safety in car maintenance tasks?
short.answer: Safety pointers are essential for both experienced mechanics and beginners to avoid injury.

short.q: What does the excerpt state is the simplest task that can become a major hurdle?
short.answer: Opening the hood, which is necessary to check oil or coolant levels.

short.q: What is the purpose of the glossary mentioned in Appendix A?
short.answer: It defines terms set in the specified font used throughout the text.

short.q: What should you do before attempting a vehicle repair yourself?
short.answer: Verify the job passes the test of time, cost, and safety, and ensure it's necessary and properly done.

short.q: What is the key tip for loosening stuck bolts according to the 'big guys'?
short.answer: Use proper tools, focus strength down arms into hands, and use longer handles for more leverage.

short.q: Why should you avoid returning to the store for incorrect parts?
short.answer: It's annoying and wastes time, as the author experienced it two out of every three times before learning proper buying tips.

short.q: What should you know before turning a repair over to a professional?
short.answer: The job's necessity, what it entails, how to verify proper completion, and how to get satisfaction if unsatisfactory.

short.q: What does the excerpt say about patience when struggling with a repair?
short.answer: Patience comes from confidence that you will eventually succeed.

short.q: What is the primary source for vehicle specifications besides the owner's manual?
short.answer: Metal tags or decals located inside the hood, often in front of the radiator or inside fenders.

short.q: What does 'OEM' mean when referring to vehicle parts?
short.answer: Original Equipment Manufacturer, meaning parts from the same brand as the vehicle's original factory.

short.q: Why should you keep a copy of the Specifications Record in your glove compartment?
short.answer: To have it readily available when shopping for parts, as the author keeps a duplicate there for convenience.

short.q: Where can you find specifications for spark plugs if not in the owner's manual?
short.answer: In a 'Tune-Up Specification Guide' (spec sheet) at an auto supply store.

short.q: What discount strategy is suggested when buying parts for DIY installation?
short.answer: Ask for a discount at auto parts stores, as professional mechanics often receive them, and it can save money without labor charges.

short.q: Why is mechanic's solvent safer than gasoline for cleaning parts?
short.answer: Mechanic's solvent works better and has been treated with a flame retardant to prevent excessive burning.

short.q: How should you handle long hair while working on a vehicle?
short.answer: Tie back long hair to prevent it from getting caught in moving parts like a fan.

short.q: What is the danger of gasoline vapors?
short.answer: Gasoline plus air forms an explosive vapor that can cause significant damage, such as destroying a neighborhood.

short.q: What must you do before running the engine to adjust something?
short.answer: Turn the engine on and off yourself to avoid the risk of a helper accidentally starting it while your hands are near moving parts.

short.q: What should you avoid when working on a vehicle's engine?
short.answer: Working on hot engine parts without checking temperature first to prevent burns.

short.q: What are the two main reasons gasoline is dangerous?
short.answer: Gasoline is toxic and flammable, and its vapor in an empty can is explosive enough to take out a city block.

short.q: How should you store toxic chemicals after use?
short.answer: Wash your hands thoroughly after using them, and either store them safely away from pets and children or dispose of them in a way that’s safe for the environment.

short.q: Why is it better to fill up gasoline in the morning?
short.answer: Gasoline expands with heat, so cooler fuel (in the morning) has a higher energy content per gallon. The sales meters are set for 60°F, so hotter fuel means you get less energy per gallon.

short.q: What is the recommended practice for working in a garage?
short.answer: Keep the garage door open and move the vehicle as close to the door as possible to ensure proper ventilation and avoid breathing in carbon monoxide or toxic fumes.

short.q: What should you use in dark areas during auto repair?
short.answer: A work light.

short.q: What should you do after the pump stops automatically and before replacing the fuel cap?
short.answer: Remove the nozzle from the fuel tank and hang it back on the pump, then replace the fuel cap while the receipt prints if using a credit card.

short.q: What is the danger of not using the vapor-recovery sleeve on the nozzle?
short.answer: Failing to depress the sleeve prevents fuel from flowing properly, as it must be pushed deeply into the filler tube to allow fuel to flow.

short.q: What is the correct action when the trigger latch is engaged?
short.answer: You can step away from the pump to avoid inhaling gasoline fumes, wash windows, or check tire pressure without holding the trigger.

short.q: Why is it important to reset the price window before pumping?
short.answer: If a previous sale is registered, the pump will not activate; resetting ensures the price window reads $0.00 to start the transaction.

short.q: What should you NOT do after the pump shuts off automatically?
short.answer: You should never 'top off' by adding fuel after the pump shuts off, as it risks overflow, fire hazards, and environmental damage.

short.q: Why is the safety catch on a car hood important?
short.answer: It prevents the hood from opening accidentally and obscuring the driver's vision while driving.

short.q: What specific cost-saving tip does the text suggest when asking for assistance to open the hood?
short.answer: Ask for $5 worth of gasoline instead of a full tank, as the cost difference is negligible.

short.q: What does the text recommend regarding checking oil?
short.answer: Check it yourself instead of having the attendant do it, as explained in a sidebar in Chapter 2.

short.q: What is the first step to open the hood according to the instructions?
short.answer: Find the hood release and pop open the hood by pressing, pushing, or pulling it.

short.q: What should you do if the hood release is at the front of the vehicle?
short.answer: Look around and through the grill, feel under the grill and behind the bumper to find a handle, lever, arm, or button.

short.q: What should you do if the hood stays up by itself after release?
short.answer: Nothing; it is acceptable as is.

short.q: What is the danger if gas leaks from hood shocks?
short.answer: The hood could come down at any moment.

short.q: Why must the rag be clean and lint-free during disassembly?
short.answer: To prevent oil, dust, or debris from fouling the parts.

short.q: What advice does the text give about rushing disassembly tasks?
short.answer: Never do a job in a hurry; allow plenty of time.

short.q: What four questions should you ask before removing a part?
short.answer: What is this thing? What does it do? How does it do it? Why is it made the way it is?

short.q: What is the key system principle for reassembling car parts?
short.answer: Placing parts on a rag in clockwise order with directional indicators shows when and how to reassemble them.

short.q: What should you note about each part during removal?
short.answer: Note how hard it was to remove, its attachment point, and direction, using numbers or sketches.

short.q: Why must parts not be over-tightened?
short.answer: Over-tightening can strip bolt threads or squeeze gaskets out of shape, causing leaks.

short.q: How do you avoid having extra nuts/bolts after a job?
short.answer: Work systematically, understand each part's function, and number/label parts during removal.

short.q: What is the main purpose of using a jack?
short.answer: To lift the car safely for tasks like changing a tire or inspecting brakes.

short.q: Why should you never use a jack to hold a vehicle in place?
short.answer: Jacks are only for lifting the vehicle off the ground and should never be used to hold it in place, as they can fail, causing serious injury or death.

short.q: What should you do if you lack wheel blocks during a flat tire?
short.answer: Park near the curb with wheels turned in, but this is still risky and not a safe substitute for proper wheel blocks.

short.q: How should you handle a flat tire on an incline?
short.answer: Park close to the curb, turn wheels toward the curb, block downside wheels securely, or seek assistance; changing the tire on an incline carries significant risk.

short.q: What is the safe procedure for a flat tire on a highway?
short.answer: Do not exit the vehicle to change the tire. Use a cellphone to call road service or hang a white rag out the window if no cellphone is available.

short.q: Why should wheel blocks be kept in the trunk?
short.answer: Keeping wheel blocks in the trunk ensures they are immediately available during an emergency tire change, avoiding the need to search for them.

short.q: Why is it unsafe to loosen lug nuts after jacking up the car?
short.answer: After jacking, the wheel turns freely, making it hard to loosen lug nuts.

short.q: What is the alternative to an owner's manual for jack placement?
short.answer: Ask the service department at a dealership.

short.q: What is the consequence of placing the jack on a bendable part?
short.answer: You can injure the car.

short.q: When must the parking brake be disengaged?
short.answer: When rotating a rear wheel or removing rear brake drums for inspection.

short.q: What is the recommended practice for beginners during two-wheel repairs?
short.answer: Stick to jacking one wheel at a time until proficient.

short.q: Why should you never use boxes, stones, or bricks as substitutes for jack stands?
short.answer: They can slip out or break while you're under the car, causing serious injury.

short.q: What is the purpose of wiggling the vehicle before removing the jack?
short.answer: To ensure the vehicle is resting securely on jack stands and the wheels are properly blocked.

short.q: What is the safest way to secure a vehicle while changing a tire?
short.answer: Use proper jack stands placed near the jack's contact point, not substitutes like boxes or stones.

short.q: How should you lower a hydraulic jack after changing a tire?
short.answer: Use the rod to turn the pressure release valve; the jack will lower itself.

short.q: What is the first step in changing a tire if you can't get cell service?
short.answer: Secure the vehicle so it won't roll, then jack it up following safe jack usage instructions.

short.q: Why is it important to check your spare tire's air pressure before trouble occurs?
short.answer: The text states to check that the spare has enough air and continue checking periodically to ensure it is usable when needed.

short.q: What should you do with lug nuts after removing them from the wheel?
short.answer: Place them into the wheel cover to prevent them from rolling away or getting lost.

short.q: How should you remove a wheel cover with a delicate finish?
short.answer: Consult the owner's manual for instructions to avoid damage; it may pry off or unscrew without causing harm.

short.q: Why should you avoid power tools for delicate lug nuts?
short.answer: Power tools can scratch the delicate finish or round off the nut corners, and they are impractical on the road without power.

short.q: What is the first step in changing a flat tire according to the excerpt?
short.answer: Remove the wheel cover or hubcap on the injured tire using a screwdriver or lug wrench flat end.

short.q: Where should you store the special key for wheel locks?
short.answer: With the vehicle.

short.q: Why must you not fully remove lug nuts before jacking up the vehicle?
short.answer: To maintain support for the tire and avoid dropping it.

short.q: What should you do to prepare for an emergency tire change?
short.answer: Practice removing lug nuts now to resolve any issues beforehand.

short.q: What is the weight handling tip when removing a flat tire?
short.answer: Support the full weight of the heavy tire until it can be lowered to the ground.

short.q: What specific tool does the text recommend buying for future lug nut loosening?
short.answer: A cross-shaft lug wrench.

short.q: Why is 'outside-in' tire repair prohibited in some states?
short.answer: Several states outlaw this procedure because it is not a proper repair method.

short.q: What is the recommended emergency fix for a flat tire when stranded?
short.answer: Use a can of nonflammable inflator/sealant to temporarily seal the puncture and get to a service station.

short.q: How should you handle inflator/sealant after using it for a flat tire?
short.answer: Get to a service station as soon as possible and inform them to remove the sealant before repairing the tire.

short.q: What should you do if the hole in a flat tire is in the sidewall?
short.answer: Do not use patches; ensure the tire is repaired properly by dismounting and fixing it correctly.

short.q: What is the purpose of run-flat tires?
short.answer: They allow driving with a flat tire to the nearest service station without damaging the tire.

short.q: Why should you not risk hiding an extra set of keys on your car?
short.answer: The text states to assume you've decided not to risk hiding an extra set of keys on your car.

short.q: What is the recommended DIY method for old-style door locks with buttons on the window ledge?
short.answer: Straighten a wire coat hanger, bend the end into a hook, and carefully hook it around the door button.

short.q: What two things does a locksmith need to make a new key?
short.answer: The key code number and personal identification.

short.q: Where should you write down the key code number for emergency access?
short.answer: Where someone at home can read it and in your pocket address book or wallet.

short.q: What is an alternative access method if you lock yourself out with the trunk open?
short.answer: Move the rear seat to gain access to the rear of the car.

true_false.q: The author believes mechanical skills are innate and cannot be learned.
Knowing how to open the hood is necessary to check oil and coolant levels.
Filling the fuel tank yourself is more expensive than full-service fueling.
The Introduction mentions the author's automotive epiphany.
Terms in the specified font are defined in Appendix A.
The excerpt states that strength for loosening bolts depends primarily on size or gender.
The author experienced needing to return to the store for incorrect parts in two out of every three jobs before learning proper buying tips.
The excerpt recommends attempting every repair yourself regardless of cost or safety.
Chapter 3 specifically covers how to buy specific tools.
The 'big guys' always found it easy to loosen stuck bolts without using leverage.
Vehicle specifications can be found on decals inside the hood near the radiator.
Aftermarket parts are always recommended over OEM parts unless personally verified.
The author believes that minor mistakes during car maintenance will cause explosions.
The Specifications Record in Appendix B should be copied for each vehicle owned.
Parts departments at dealerships can look up specifications for parts if you don't have a service manual.
Working on a vehicle is completely free from danger.
You can work on a vehicle with the parking brake off.
Mechanic's solvent contains a flame retardant.
It is safe to store gasoline in a regular can for long periods.
You should push wrenches when loosening stuck parts.
You should store gasoline in any container.
The vapor in an empty gasoline can is not explosive.
It is safe to carry a gasoline can in your vehicle for short trips.
You should work in a closed garage without ventilation when running the engine.
Filling up gasoline in the morning is recommended because gas stations are less crowded.
You must insert your credit card before selecting the grade of gasoline.
Topping off a tank saves money by filling it completely.
Gas pumps automatically shut off when the tank is almost full.
Hot weather causes fuel to contract, reducing overflow risk.
The trigger latch requires you to hold the trigger continuously while pumping.
The hood release location is identical for all vehicle models.
The safety catch prevents the hood from opening accidentally while driving.
The cost difference for $5 versus a full tank of gas is significant.
The text advises letting a gas station attendant check your oil for you.
After popping the hood, you can immediately lift it all the way open without the safety catch.
If the hood stays up all by itself, no further action is needed.
A hood prop is always a long, thin metal rod.
Gas-pressurized hood shocks require immediate replacement if leaking.
The rag used for disassembly should be placed in an area where oil can fall on it.
The text states that you can skip asking 'Why is it made the way it is?' before disassembly.
Parts should be laid on the rag in counterclockwise order during removal.
Reassembly should begin with the last part removed.
This book covers exact torque specifications for parts.
Numbering parts with masking tape is recommended if a rag might move.
Jack stands are essential when jacking a car.
Jacks can be safely used to hold a vehicle in place while working under it.
Using jack stands is optional when working under a vehicle.
Changing a tire on a highway is safe and recommended.
Wheel blocks should be made of bricks, wooden wedges, or metal chocks.
If you have no cellphone, you should change the tire on the side of the road.
Working under a car without secure jacking and blocking is safe.
The parking brake should be engaged when inspecting brakes by removing rear brake drums.
It is acceptable to place the jack on parts that can bend.
After jacking, the wheel turns freely, making lug nuts easier to loosen.
For two-wheel repairs, you can immediately place the jack near the rear axle without practice.
Using a scissor jack requires turning the crank in the opposite direction to lower the vehicle.
It is safe to use bricks as temporary jack stands when changing a tire.
Wiggling the vehicle checks if the wheels are properly blocked.
The textbook recommends calling for help before attempting to change a tire roadside.
Jack stands should be placed far from where the jack touched the vehicle.
You should check your spare tire's location only after experiencing a flat tire.
The text recommends checking the spare tire's air pressure periodically.
Power tools with rubberized fittings are practical for removing delicate lug nuts on the road.
Lug nuts with delicate finishes can be safely loosened with power tools.
The excerpt states to lay the wheel cover on its back to keep lug nuts from rolling away.
Wheel locks require a special key provided with the car to unlock them.
Garages typically retighten lug nuts with a power tool, making them difficult to loosen by hand.
A hollow pipe can be safely used on lug nuts with special finishes to loosen them.
You should fully remove lug nuts before jacking up the vehicle.
When loosening lug nuts, you must work in consecutive order to avoid missing any.
Patches can be safely used for sidewall punctures.
Inflator/sealant should be used as a permanent tire repair solution.
Run-flat tires are less expensive than standard tires.
Service stations are allowed to plug flats from the outside in all states.
It is safe to drive indefinitely with a flat tire repaired using inflator/sealant.
Electronic door openers prevent you from locking the doors with keys inside the vehicle.
The wire coat hanger technique works for all types of door locks.
Dealers always have the key code number on file for every vehicle they sold.
A locksmith can make a new key using only the key code number.
Some vehicles have an override switch to start the ignition without the electronic door opener.
